title: Authors and Citation
text: Muschelli J (2026). asleep: Estimate Sleep 'Accelerometry' Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/jhuwit/asleep.
code: @Manual{,   title = {asleep: Estimate Sleep from 'Accelerometry' Data},   author = {John Muschelli},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/jhuwit/asleep}, }

title: Estimate Sleep from Accelerometry Data
text: goal asleep wrap https://github.com/OxWearables/asleep algorithm.

title: Estimate Sleep from Accelerometry Data
text: See https://github.com/OxWearables/asleep?tab=readme-ov-file#install install asleep python module. R, can via: finished, able check via:
code: envname = "asleep" reticulate::conda_create(envname = envname,                           python_version = "3.8") reticulate::use_condaenv(envname) reticulate::py_install(c("asleep", "argparse", "numpy", "pandas"),                         envname = envname,                        method = "conda",                        python_version = "3.8",                        pip = TRUE) envname = "asleep" reticulate::use_condaenv(envname) asleep::have_asleep()

title: Estimate Sleep from Accelerometry Data
text: main function asleep::asleep, takes can take file directly: Let’s see inside output, list values, namely data.frame outputs: main caveat asleep precise format data, primarily must columns time, x, y, z data.

title: Estimate Sleep from Accelerometry Data
text: Alternatively, can pass data.frame, rename columns need run asleep : gives output data:

what: Run asleep Model on Data — asleep
title: Run asleep Model on Data — asleep
text: Run asleep Model Data

title: Run asleep Model on Data — asleep
code: asleep(   file,   outdir = NULL,   min_wear_hours = 22L,   time_shift = "0",   report_light_and_temp = FALSE,   pytorch_device = c("cpu", "cuda:0"),   verbose = TRUE,   force_download = FALSE )

title: Run asleep Model on Data — asleep
text: file accelerometry file process, including CSV, CWA, GT3X, GENEActiv bin files outdir output directory CSVs outputs min_wear_hours Min wear time hours eligible summary statistics computation. sleepnet paper uses 22 time_shift number hours shift forward backward current device time. e.g. +1 -1 report_light_and_temp true, adds mean temp, light columns predictions pytorch_device device use prediction PyTorch. verbose print diagnostic messages force_download force download model, passed sl_download_models()

title: Run asleep Model on Data — asleep
text: list outputs, including summaries, paths, dataframes.

title: Run asleep Model on Data — asleep
code: # \donttest{   file = system.file("extdata/example_sleep.csv.gz", package = "asleep")   stopifnot(file.exists(file))   if (asleep_check()) {     sl_download_models()     out = try({asleep(file = file, verbose = 2L)})     if (inherits(out, "try-error")) {       message(out)       reticulate::py_last_error()     } else {       pred = out$predictions     }   } #> Downloading uv... #> Done! #> Checking Data #> File is:/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/asleep/extdata/example_sleep.csv.gz #> Downloading models if not already present #> Parsing raw data #> Transforming data for model input #> Data shape for data2model: (480, 3, 900) #> Data shape for times: (480,) #> Data shape for nonwear: (480,) #> Detecting sleep windows #> Running SleepNet #> Mapping SleepNet predictions back to original time series #> Generating predictions dataframe #> Generating sleep block df and indicate the longest block per day #> Generating daily summary statistics #> Creating outputs # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   file = system.file("extdata/example_sleep.csv.gz", package = "asleep")   df = readr::read_csv(file)   if (asleep_check()) {     out = asleep(file = df)     st = out$predictions   }   if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE) &&       requireNamespace("tidyr", quietly = TRUE) &&       requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) {     d = st[1:250,] %>% dplyr::mutate(time = lubridate::as_datetime(time))     raw = df %>% dplyr::filter(time >= min(d$time) & time <= max(d$time))     dat = raw %>%       tidyr::gather(axis, value, -time)     #dat %>%     #   ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = time, y = value, colour = axis)) +     #   ggplot2::geom_line() +     #   ggplot2::geom_segment(data = d, ggplot2::aes(xintercept = time))   }  } # }

title: Load asleep Model for Sleep Window Detector — sl_load_model
text: model_path file path model.  disk, can re-used re-downloaded.  NULL, download temporary directory force_download force download model, even already exists model_path as_python Keep model object python object ... sl_download_model, additional arguments pass curl::curl_download()

text: file path file reading resample_hz Target frequency (Hz) resample signal. "uniform", use implied frequency (use option fix device sampling errors). Pass NULL disable. Defaults "uniform". keep_pandas convert data data.frame keep pandas data.frame
